rationale: In the literature review, the cited paper is not only listed but specifically characterized as among the first to introduce LDA to marketing for user-generated content. This role supports the citing paper's methodological framing and novelty claim relative to prior marketing topic-model applications. The citation is therefore more than a passing mention, even though the citing study does not directly reuse the cited paper's exact empirical setting or full framework.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Tirunillai and Tellis (2014) as an early marketing precedent for applying LDA/topic modeling to consumer-generated text. It then positions its own contribution as building on that topic-modeling stream by integrating text topics with channel-visit and purchase-state modeling.
